title: Tonglen (Giving & Receiving)
blurb: Breathe in suffering, breathe out relief — a compassion practice.
lead: Tonglen is a Tibetan Buddhist compassion practice: breathing in the pain of yourself or others, and breathing out relief and care.

blocks.text: Tonglen reverses our usual instinct. On the in-breath, you willingly take in suffering (your own or others'); on the out-breath, you send out relief, spaciousness, and kindness.
It's a powerful practice for cultivating compassion and working with difficult emotions. As a contemplative practice, it is rooted in tradition rather than clinical study.

items: Sit quietly and take a few settling breaths.
Bring to mind suffering — your own, or someone else's.
As you breathe in, imagine taking in that pain, willing to feel it.
As you breathe out, send relief, warmth, ease, and kindness.
Continue, widening to others who suffer in similar ways.

blocks.text: Fearing you'll 'absorb' harm. Tonglen is imaginative compassion training — it builds courage and warmth, not real danger.

resources.title: How to practice Tonglen
resources.whyHere: Clear free guidance from a renowned teacher.
